name: OECS Revised Treaty of Basseterre 2010 (Organisation of Eastern Caribbean States)
summary: The Revised Treaty of Basseterre Establishing the Organisation of Eastern Caribbean States Economic Union (OECS Revised Treaty) is a regional supranational meta-framework foundational-OECS-treaty framework adopted by the 6 founding Eastern Caribbean states in Saint Lucia — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Eastern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal Eastern-Caribbean-Economic-Union framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Economic-Union framework with formal monetary-union framework via Eastern Caribbean Currency Union ECCU framework (OECS Revised Treaty signed 18 June 2010 in Saint Lucia + effective 21 January 2011 + original Treaty of Basseterre signed 18 June 1981 in Basseterre Saint Kitts and Nevis founding OECS — formal foundational revised OECS treaty framework establishing comprehensive OECS organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-regional-Caribbean-treaty frameworks which operate on free-trade-area or common-market frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Eastern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Economic-Union framework + only modern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal monetary-union framework via Eastern Caribbean Currency Union ECCU + Eastern Caribbean Central Bank ECCB framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 6-independent-states + 3-British-Overseas-Territories-mixed-membership framework + smallest regional supranational binding-treaty framework by population framework with ~600,000 combined population framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal OECS Economic Union framework (OECS Revised Treaty Protocol on Economic Union providing formal Economic Union framework operating five freedoms: free movement of goods + services + capital + persons + harmonisation of fiscal + monetary + trade policies — formal foundational Eastern Caribbean Economic-Union framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Eastern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Economic-Union framework with five-freedoms framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Eastern Caribbean Currency Union ECCU framework (Eastern Caribbean Currency Union ECCU established 1965 + Eastern Caribbean Central Bank ECCB established 1 October 1983 in Basseterre Saint Kitts and Nevis providing formal Eastern Caribbean Dollar EC$ currency framework pegged to USD framework — formal foundational Eastern Caribbean Currency Union framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal monetary-union framework with Eastern Caribbean Dollar pegged to USD framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 60-year-monetary-union framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal OECS Citizenship by Investment framework (OECS Citizenship by Investment CBI programmes effective from Saint Kitts and Nevis 1984 + Antigua and Barbuda 2013 + Dominica 1993 + Grenada 2013 + Saint Lucia 2015 — formal foundational Caribbean Citizenship-by-Investment framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal multi-state-Citizenship-by-Investment framework + only modern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Citizenship-by-Investment framework). The OECS Revised Treaty is binding upon 6 OECS member states + 4 OECS associate members as of 2026 (Antigua and Barbuda + Dominica + Grenada + Montserrat + Saint Kitts and Nevis + Saint Lucia + Saint Vincent and the Grenadines + 3 British Overseas Territories Anguilla + Martinique + British Virgin Islands) — comprehensive Eastern Caribbean regional coverage framework. The Revised Treaty is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal OECS Economic Union framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Eastern-Caribbean-regional cross-border-children's-rights framework + free-movement-of-persons framework. The OECS Authority of Heads of Government + OECS Council of Ministers + OECS Commission + OECS Assembly + Eastern Caribbean Central Bank ECCB are the formal OECS bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies (Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court ECSC as final court of appeal for 6 OECS members + JCPC as final court of appeal for remaining OECS states). The OECS Revised Treaty is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: Treaty of Basseterre (Original)
statutory_framework.relevance: Original Treaty of Basseterre signed 18 June 1981 in Basseterre Saint Kitts and Nevis founding OECS — predecessor framework to OECS Revised Treaty.

statutory_framework.title: OECS Revised Treaty
statutory_framework.relevance: Revised Treaty of Basseterre Establishing the Organisation of Eastern Caribbean States Economic Union signed 18 June 2010 in Saint Lucia + effective 21 January 2011 — formal foundational revised OECS treaty framework establishing comprehensive OECS organisation framework.

statutory_framework.citation: OECS Revised Treaty Protocol on Economic Union
statutory_framework.title: OECS Economic Union Protocol
statutory_framework.relevance: OECS Revised Treaty Protocol on Economic Union providing formal Economic Union framework with five freedoms framework — formal foundational Eastern Caribbean Economic-Union framework.

statutory_framework.title: ECCU
statutory_framework.relevance: Eastern Caribbean Currency Union ECCU established 1965 + Eastern Caribbean Central Bank ECCB established 1 October 1983 in Basseterre Saint Kitts and Nevis — formal foundational Eastern Caribbean Currency Union framework.

statutory_framework.title: Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court
statutory_framework.relevance: Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court ECSC established 27 February 1967 in Castries Saint Lucia providing formal OECS judicial framework as final court of appeal for 6 OECS members — formal foundational OECS judicial framework.

apex_courts.name: OECS Authority of Heads of Government
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for OECS.

apex_courts.name: Eastern Caribbean Central Bank (ECCB)
apex_courts.role: Formal Eastern Caribbean Currency Union framework body.

apex_courts.name: Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court (ECSC)
apex_courts.role: Final court of appeal for 6 OECS members framework body.

anonymisation_convention: OECS-related communications operate via OECS Authority of Heads of Government framework + ECSC framework; individual communications limited under OECS framework.

key_developments.title: Eastern Caribbean Currency Union established
key_developments.description: Eastern Caribbean Currency Union ECCU established 1965 — predecessor framework to OECS Treaty.

key_developments.title: Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court established
key_developments.description: Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court ECSC established 27 February 1967 in Castries Saint Lucia — formal foundational OECS judicial framework operational.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Basseterre signed + OECS founded
key_developments.description: Original Treaty of Basseterre signed 18 June 1981 in Basseterre Saint Kitts and Nevis founding OECS — formal foundational original OECS treaty framework adopted.

key_developments.title: Eastern Caribbean Central Bank established
key_developments.description: Eastern Caribbean Central Bank ECCB established 1 October 1983 in Basseterre Saint Kitts and Nevis — formal foundational ECCB framework operational.

key_developments.title: Saint Kitts and Nevis Citizenship by Investment programme
key_developments.description: Saint Kitts and Nevis Citizenship by Investment CBI programme effective 1984 — formal foundational Caribbean Citizenship-by-Investment framework + first global modern CBI programme.

key_developments.title: OECS Revised Treaty signed
key_developments.description: Revised Treaty of Basseterre Establishing the Organisation of Eastern Caribbean States Economic Union signed 18 June 2010 in Saint Lucia — formal foundational revised OECS treaty framework adopted.

key_developments.title: OECS Revised Treaty enters force + OECS Economic Union
key_developments.description: OECS Revised Treaty entered force 21 January 2011 + OECS Economic Union framework operational — formal foundational revised OECS treaty + Economic Union framework operational.

key_developments.title: Saint Lucia joins CCJ Appellate Jurisdiction + OECS sustainability
key_developments.description: Saint Lucia replaced JCPC with CCJ Appellate Jurisdiction 2024 framework + OECS-Trade-Sustainability framework 2024 — formal OECS framework expansion.

structural_findings: OECS Revised Treaty operates a regional supranational binding-treaty framework — places OECS Revised Treaty in the regional supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern Eastern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal Eastern-Caribbean-Economic-Union framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Economic-Union framework with formal monetary-union framework via Eastern Caribbean Currency Union ECCU framework + only modern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal monetary-union framework via ECCU framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 6-independent-states + 3-British-Overseas-Territories-mixed-membership framework + smallest regional supranational binding-treaty framework by population framework with ~600,000 combined population framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal OECS Economic Union framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Eastern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Economic-Union framework with five-freedoms framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Eastern Caribbean Currency Union ECCU framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal monetary-union framework with Eastern Caribbean Dollar pegged to USD framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 60-year-monetary-union framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal OECS Citizenship by Investment framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal multi-state-Citizenship-by-Investment framework + only modern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Citizenship-by-Investment framework.

6 OECS member state + 4 OECS associate member ratification framework with ~600,000 combined population is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive Eastern Caribbean regional coverage framework.

1965 ECCU + 1967 ECSC + 1981 Treaty of Basseterre + 1983 ECCB + 1984 Saint Kitts CBI + 2010 OECS Revised Treaty + 2011 entry into force + 2024 Saint Lucia CCJ + OECS Trade Sustainability OECS-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

OECS Revised Treaty + Treaty of Basseterre + Protocol on Economic Union + ECCU + ECCB + ECSC + Citizenship by Investment formal septet framework with formal Eastern-Caribbean-regional cross-border-children's-rights PA-intersection framework via free-movement-of-persons framework is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: OECS Revised Treaty jurisdiction sidecar — regional supranational Eastern Caribbean foundational-OECS-treaty meta-framework (OECS Revised Treaty 2010/2011 + original Treaty of Basseterre 1981 + Protocol on Economic Union + ECCU 1965 + ECCB 1983 + ECSC 1967 + Saint Kitts CBI 1984 + Saint Lucia CCJ 2024). Only modern Eastern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework globally + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Economic-Union framework with formal monetary-union framework via ECCU + only modern Caribbean regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal monetary-union framework + smallest regional supranational binding-treaty framework by population ~600,000 + central jurisdiction of formal OECS Economic Union five-freedoms framework + central jurisdiction of formal Eastern Caribbean Currency Union 60-year-monetary-union framework + central jurisdiction of formal multi-state Citizenship-by-Investment framework + 6 OECS member states + 4 associate members + 1965 ECCU + 1967 ECSC + 1981 Treaty of Basseterre + 1983 ECCB + 1984 Saint Kitts CBI + 2010 OECS Revised Treaty + 2011 entry into force + 2024 Saint Lucia CCJ + OECS Trade Sustainability OECS-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: OECS Economic Union framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Eastern-Caribbean-regional cross-border-children's-rights framework + free-movement-of-persons framework.

Joins regional + supranational + Eastern-Caribbean-foundational-OECS-treaty cluster + OECS-Revised-Treaty-only-Eastern-Caribbean-regional-supranational-binding-treaty-globally-distinctive + Economic-Union-five-freedoms + Eastern-Caribbean-Currency-Union-1965 + Eastern-Caribbean-Central-Bank-ECCB-Basseterre + Eastern-Caribbean-Supreme-Court-ECSC-Castries + Eastern-Caribbean-Dollar-pegged-USD + 6-OECS-member-states + 4-OECS-associate-members + 3-British-Overseas-Territories-mixed-membership + smallest-regional-supranational-by-population-600000 + Saint-Kitts-CBI-1984-first-modern-CBI-programme + multi-state-Citizenship-by-Investment + 60-year-monetary-union + 1981-Treaty-of-Basseterre-original + 2010-OECS-Revised-Treaty + 2024-Saint-Lucia-CCJ-Appellate + Eastern-Caribbean-cross-border-children-rights-PA-intersection-via-five-freedoms clusters within the corpus.
